political.s: NordicTrack/iFIT is a private company with no identified PAC or lobbying activities in federal filings. The company has not been documented in major political advocacy records. No significant political donations or lobbying expenditures have been disclosed.

charity.s: The company donated 1.3 million medical-grade surgical masks to Utah during COVID-19. iFIT has an employee-founded nonprofit foundation established in 2009 that supports local communities, schools, and organizations. However, overall charitable giving remains limited and not substantially documented.
charity.focus: COVID-19 relief, local community support, education

environment.s: No significant environmental sustainability initiatives, carbon reduction targets, or environmental certifications have been publicly documented. Manufacturing and supply chain environmental practices are not disclosed.

labor.s: Employees report difficult workplace experiences and customer service issues are widespread. Multiple lawsuits allege product defects and poor customer support. The company has faced criticism regarding service delays and quality control problems in manufacturing and support.

dei.s: No specific DEI initiatives, diversity reports, or equal employment opportunity documentation have been publicly disclosed. The company has not published a formal DEI statement or diversity metrics.

animals.s: Not applicable. NordicTrack manufactures fitness equipment and software, not products involving animal testing or animal-derived components.

guns.s: Not applicable. NordicTrack manufactures fitness equipment and does not produce firearms or weapons.

privacy.s: Mozilla Foundation rates NordicTrack privacy practices as poor. The platform collects extensive personal data including workout history, health metrics, and device diagnostics. Users can be contacted for marketing via phone/email even if on do-not-call lists. While the company updated privacy policies to limit outright data sales, data sharing for digital tracking and third-party service providers remains extensive.

execPay.s: CEO Scott Watterson founded the company and maintains operational control. Specific executive compensation ratios are not disclosed as the company is private. No SEC filings available for compensation analysis.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
